claim_a: Passwords must be rotated every 90 days.
claim_b: Passwords must be rotated every 180 days.
rationale: Same subject (passwords), conflicting timeframes (90 vs 180 days).

claim_a: MFA is mandatory for all employees.
claim_b: MFA is optional for all employees.
rationale: Same subject (MFA for all employees), mutually exclusive obligations.

claim_a: Security exceptions are valid for 12 months and must be re-approved.
claim_b: Security exceptions are valid for 6 months and must be re-approved.
rationale: Same subject (exception validity period), conflicting durations.

claim_a: Employees may work remotely up to 2 days per week.
claim_b: Employees may work remotely up to 5 days per week.
rationale: Same subject (remote work policy), conflicting limits.

claim_a: VPN is required for all remote access to corporate resources.
claim_b: VPN is optional when accessing cloud-hosted applications remotely.
rationale: Overlapping scope (remote access), conflicting VPN requirements.

claim_a: All data at rest must be encrypted using AES-256.
claim_b: Data at rest encryption is not required for non-production environments.
rationale: Scope-exception: 'All data at rest' has a carve-out for non-production environments. Both rules can be simultaneously true.

claim_a: User sessions must time out after 15 minutes of inactivity.
claim_b: User sessions must time out after 60 minutes of inactivity.
rationale: Same subject (user sessions), conflicting timeout values.

claim_a: Third-party vendors must undergo security review annually.
claim_b: Third-party vendors must undergo security review quarterly.
rationale: Same subject (vendor security review), conflicting frequency.

claim_a: All production changes require CAB approval before deployment.
claim_b: Emergency changes may be deployed without prior CAB approval.
rationale: Scope-exception: Emergency changes are a valid carve-out to the standard change approval process.

claim_a: System logs must be retained for 365 days.
claim_b: Database backups must be retained for 90 days.
rationale: Different subjects: system logs vs database backups.

claim_a: Data in transit must use TLS 1.2 or higher.
claim_b: TLS 1.0 and 1.1 are blocked on all endpoints.
rationale: Compatible — blocking old TLS supports requiring 1.2+.

claim_a: Standard user passwords must be minimum 14 characters.
claim_b: Privileged user passwords must be minimum 18 characters.
rationale: Different account classes: standard vs privileged.

claim_a: Privileged sessions time out after 15 minutes of inactivity.
claim_b: Read-only sessions time out after 45 minutes of inactivity.
rationale: Different session types: privileged vs read-only.

claim_a: All production microservices must use OAuth2 for authentication.
claim_b: Multi-factor authentication is optional for all employees.
rationale: Different subjects: microservices auth vs employee MFA.

claim_a: Access is provisioned based on least privilege principle.
claim_b: All new accounts are provisioned through an automated landing zone.
rationale: Complementary controls — least privilege and landing zone are compatible.

claim_a: All PII must be encrypted at rest using AES-256.
claim_b: Customer data is stored in PostgreSQL databases.
rationale: Non-contradictory — encryption policy and storage technology are independent.

claim_a: Passwords must be rotated every 180 days.
claim_b: Passwords are hashed using bcrypt with a cost factor of 12.
rationale: Complementary — rotation policy and hashing algorithm are independent.

claim_a: All security events must be forwarded to the SIEM within 5 minutes.
claim_b: The SOC team must acknowledge critical incidents within 15 minutes.
rationale: Different stages of incident pipeline — forwarding vs acknowledgement.

claim_a: All vendor contracts must include a 99.99% uptime SLA.
claim_b: Vendor contracts are required to guarantee at least 99.9% uptime.
rationale: Conflicting strict requirements for the same subject (vendor SLA uptime).

claim_a: Financial records must be permanently deleted after 7 years.
claim_b: Financial records must be retained indefinitely for audit purposes.
rationale: Same subject (financial records), mutually exclusive retention policies (deleted after 7 years vs retained indefinitely).

claim_a: All personnel must complete annual security awareness training.
claim_b: Temporary contractors with less than 30-day contracts are exempt from annual security training.
rationale: Scope-exception: Contractors with <30 day contracts are a specific carve-out to the general personnel rule.

claim_a: The maximum allowable expense for a client dinner is $100 per head.
claim_b: Executive leadership may approve client dinners up to $250 per head.
rationale: Scope-exception: Executive leadership has a specific override/carve-out for the general expense limit.

claim_a: All software vulnerabilities must be patched within 30 days of discovery.
claim_b: Critical severity vulnerabilities must be patched within 7 days of discovery.
rationale: Ambiguous edge case: One is a stricter subset of the other. Following the 7-day rule for criticals does not make it impossible to follow the 30-day rule for all vulnerabilities. They can coexist.
